hints.Accusative: Accusative neuter is the same form, but 'bonum' is the subject — predicate of 'est'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'boni' — 'bonum' is nominative here
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'bono' — 'bonum' is nominative subject

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'bonum' with -um is 2nd declension neuter (substantivized adjective)
hints.3rd: 3rd declension neuter nouns have varied endings — 'bonum, boni' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'bonum' is 2nd declension

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'erat' — 'est' is present indicative

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'est' here is from 'esse'
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not related
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — different verb

hints.to call: 'to call' is 'appellare' — do not confuse with 'appetere'
hints.to reach: 'to reach' is 'attingere' or 'petere' — 'appetere' specifically means 'to strive for / desire'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'appetant' — 'appetunt' with -unt is indicative
hints.Future indicative: Future would be 'appetent' — 'appetunt' is present indicative

hints.appello, appellare, appellavi, appellatu: These are the parts of 'appellare' (to call) — a different verb
hints.appareo, apparere, apparui, apparitum: These are the parts of 'apparere' (to appear) — not related
hints.peto, petere, petivi, petitum: These are the parts of 'petere' (to seek) — the simple verb; 'appetere' is its compound

translation: The good is that which all things desire.
